data.body:
Scope expansion (2026-04-26, from docs/proposed-mitigations-4-26.md):

Two mitigations absorbed into this epic's experimental-verification scope:

- **G2-M2** — When a planned task touches plugins/dso/scripts/runners/, mandatorily emit a paired sub-task: 'Update the runner-equivalence corpus in tests/runners/ and add a RED test asserting both bash-runner.sh and suite-engine.sh produce identical classification.' This sub-task cannot be merged with another task. Receiving home for the corpus is delivered by New Epic D (cross-runner equivalence contract testing) per docs/new-epics-4-26.md.
- **G4-M3** — When a planned task is classified as touching shell hooks, runners, or platform-sensitive scripts, the task spec auto-includes reviewer flags (correctness:hermeticity, correctness:platform). The reviewer agent reads these flags and applies the corresponding rubric items at higher severity. Rubric items themselves are delivered by New Epic B (reviewer rubric expansion).

Rationale: this epic already establishes the principle of forcing experimental verification of integration assumptions at planning time. Runner equivalence (G2-M2) is structurally a verification-before-coding pattern for a different domain. Reviewer flag injection (G4-M3) is a planner-to-reviewer signal, the planning-phase counterpart to G2-M2's task generation.

This epic appears to have no children yet (pre-manifest); decomposition will pick up the new SCs naturally.
